title: kernel_swarm_test_harness primitive (N concurrent governed sessions)
goal: Per spec §6.7.1 (AC-SWARM-HARNESS-PRIMITIVE) build the `kernel_swarm_test_harness` primitive that spawns N concurrent governed sessions against the same Handshake instance with no shared session state between concurrent sessions (each session independent and re-entrant).
expected_diff_shape: New module src/backend/handshake_core/src/test_harness/{mod.rs,swarm.rs,session.rs}; SwarmHarness struct + per-session worker; tests for N=2 baseline (real N=8 lands in MT-035).
unblock_conditions: MT-007 ProcessLedger + MT-008 reclaim ship (each spawned session writes to ledger); MT-020 automation-first audit (swarm sessions inherit quiet-mode guarantees); MT-027 visual smoke + MT-030 replay-drive (sessions exercise the mutation surface during swarm).
implementation_notes: Per spec §6.7.1. API: `SwarmHarness::new(n: usize, scenario: SwarmScenario) -> SwarmHarness` and `.run() -> SwarmReport`. SwarmScenario is a trait `trait SwarmScenario { fn session_steps(&self, session_idx: usize) -> Vec<SessionStep>; }` where SessionStep enum carries the per-step actions (e.g., OpenWorkspace { ws_id }, MutateViaCatalog { action_id, envelope }, ReadInspector, CloseSession). The harness: (1) for each session 0..N, spawn a tokio task with its own SessionId (UUID v7); (2) each session runs through its SessionStep list independently; (3) sessions write to a SHARED EventLedger + ProcessLedger but have NO shared in-process state (re-entrance guarantee — assert no thread_local! used outside test infra); (4) on session end, write to SwarmReport. SwarmReport: `{ n, scenario_id, sessions: Vec<SessionResult { session_id, steps_completed, errors, duration_ms, process_ledger_rows }>, total_duration_ms, contention_events: Vec<ContentionEvent>, ledger_overflow_count: u64 }`. The harness wires into MT-007 ledger writer (so overflow is observable in the report) and MT-008 reclaim (test for session-close path covers all four ReclaimTriggers). Tests: N=2 baseline — assert both sessions complete independently, no shared-state leak (verify via thread_local detection trick: each session sets a per-session marker and asserts it does not appear in the other session's read view). Real N=8 perf testing lands in MT-035.
risk_if_missed: Without the swarm harness, HBR-SWARM-001/002/003/004 have no test surface; concurrency invariants are unverified.
validator_focus: Sessions truly independent (no thread_local! leak); SwarmReport captures contention events explicitly; ProcessLedger rows correlate session_id correctly under concurrency.

minimum_controls: Sessions are truly independent (per-session marker test).
SwarmReport captures contention events as structured rows.
Session IDs use Uuid::now_v7() per HBR-INT-008.

integration_validator_review.verdict_reason: SwarmHarness::new(n, scenario).run() spawns N independent tokio tasks per session with UUID v7 session ids, each session carries its own SessionLocalState marker, the source-tree scan rejects thread_local!, and the baseline test asserts both sessions complete with no foreign_marker leak and matched ProcessLedger Start/Stop rows.

integration_validator_review_prior.verdict_reason: SwarmHarness::new(n, scenario).run() spawns N independent tokio tasks per session with UUID v7 session ids, each session carries its own SessionLocalState marker, the source-tree scan rejects thread_local!, and the baseline test asserts both sessions complete with no foreign_marker leak and matched ProcessLedger Start/Stop rows.
